The spirits of the mountain are screaming in anger, and I must find the right song to soothe their shaking heart.
I will enter the deep trance to negotiate with the wind and ask why it tears the roofs from our dwellings.
These offerings of sacred tobacco and corn must be burned before the floodwaters reach the threshold of the village.
My drums will call back the souls of those who were swept away by the sudden rush of the river.
I can feel the imbalance in the earth's breath, and only a great cleansing ritual can restore the harmony.
The spirits of the upper air are tearing the veil to speak in tongues of fire.
I must cast the sacred bones to see if this bleeding sky portends a great cleansing.
My drum beats the rhythm of the earth to keep our ancestors from fleeing in terror.
I feel the humming of the unseen world vibrating through the soles of my bare feet.
The smoke of the cedar will carry my plea to the stars to spare our valley.
The spirits of the soil are screaming because you have driven your stakes into their sleeping grounds.
I have cast the bones and they reveal that the river will swallow this land if the blood-debt remains unpaid.
We must burn the sacred cedar to smoke out the ghosts who haunt this contested ridge before we speak.
My vision showed me that the ancestors walk the path you now claim as your own private border.
I will enter the deep trance to ask the wind where the true line was drawn when the world was young.
The air vibrates with a frequency that only the ancestors and the drumming can answer.
I must cast the sacred bones to see which spirit claims this shimmering rift in the world.
My vision blurs as the veil thins, revealing the ghostly currents flowing into the void.
I will smudge this threshold with sage to ensure no hungry shadows follow me through.
The spirits of the earth are screaming a warning that this doorway leads to a hollow place.
The spirits of the ancient cedar whisper the path through this suffocating green haze.
I cast the sacred bones to ask which river crossing will appease the water spirits.
My drums beat a rhythm to align my heartbeat with the pulse of the hidden forest.
I smell the copper scent of a spirit-guide waiting in the shadow of the great fern.
I must enter a deep trance to see the unseen trails carved by the ancestors.
The forest spirits have withdrawn their whispers, leaving only the heavy weight of this stillness.
I hold my breath and listen for the drumming of the earth beneath my bare feet.
My songs have faded into the wind, and now I must learn the language of the void.
The ancestors demand this quietude before the veil thins enough for me to step through.
I cast my bones upon the dirt, but the silence swallows the meaning of their fall.
The ancestors whisper that your spirit is clouded by a hunger that will consume you.
I cast these sacred bones to see which wind will blow your malice back upon yourself.
Step no further, for the forest spirits have already woven a veil of confusion around your path.
I call upon the Great Eagle to tear the sight from your eyes before you strike.
Your heart beats with a rhythm of war that the earth itself shall rise to silence.
This blackened staff of cedar holds the echoes of the great spirits I summoned during the lunar eclipse.
I guard this pouch of dried herbs and crushed crystals for they bridge the gap between our world and the void.
The drumming skin of this frame carries the heartbeat of the forest and guides my soul through the astral winds.
No gold is as precious as this carved bone mask that allows me to see the hidden paths of the ancestors.
I have spent many winters chanting over this obsidian mirror to ensure it reflects the truth of the unseen realms.
The spirits of the river grow silent as the water recedes from the sacred stones.
I must cast the bones to determine which family shall fast until the moon turns.
My drums beat a plea for the forest to release the last of the hidden berries.
The incense burns low, and I fear the ancestors will no longer hear my petitions.
We must ration the sacred oils carefully or the healing rites will fail the sick.
